reviews.review_description: when playing on the Virtual pool   and shooters pool on the PC  you push on  (M) button to move around  with the mouse  and The (S) button for the zoom ...when Developers agree to use a options like that in Pool  and Snooker..then and only then these games will bee good and probably more popular

This is a fun pool game with many different environments and cue sticks to choose from. Personally I haven’t had any issues, it has good physics, colorful graphics and am having a blast playing it! Good job guys!

This app feels almost completely abandoned. It’s nearly unplayable due to constant disconnects and multiplayer invite issues. You can’t even use your own avatar anymore and are forced to choose from a few poorly made premade options. This used to be my favorite VR game, but it seems like the developers have forgotten about it entirely. I can’t recommend purchasing this in its current state.

Can't place white ball glitch, Mixed reality mode might be cool but you can't turn it on and access the settings for mixed reality again. Stuck and couldn't shoot ball in mixed reality. The aiming has a slight ghosting/slow catch up, probably to help people lock their shot but it's kinda bad too. 

Some what a good game but loads of bugs make it feel really bad.

Due to hateful/abusive DMs I keep recieving from other users because of my reviews, im now editing my reviews to keep it short, 

Its a enjoyable game for what it is, but its not for those super pool enthusiasts though
At first fun game now cant play match stuck at loading screen report to dev mant time for we get back its now 2 week can't play gj

£14 a game I don’t even use it so don’t buy it
￼

Would be great if it wasn’t so glitchy. Honestly, it seems like the developers have all but abandoned this app. Sometimes you can’t even finish a game without it freezing up.  
Went for about a year with avatars in the game looking like something out of a possession. 
Now my brothers says my avatar looks like John Leguizamo…athere’s no way to fix it.

developer_response.body:
Hello NunyaBiz1217, Can you please reach out to our support team so we can investigate the freezing issue you are experiencing? Thank you.
(link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
You should be able to edit your avatar before launching the game. Please let our support team know if you have any questions. 
Thank you, Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: It would be more useful to me, if it were not “Internet Connection Required”…

Already owned darts and Cornhill.  I wanted
 a pool game for casual play and opted for this .It's great that it contains the jukebox again and I can just chill . The game plays well . I have had zero issues with it . I look forward to trying out multi player .

Thank you so much for your awesome review C.Y.B.E.R.M.A.N! We hope you continue to have a great time listening to the jukebox and playing pool! 
Game on!
Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: I would not be surprised if "time" in this game I dont have more hours than anyone.  Being able to listen to YouTube music together and enjoy pool has practically become a nightly thing.  Love this game.   But for gods sake please fix how 80% of time in 9ball if the host breaks it makes a crazy noise and then the balls split up before the host even goes.  Been doing this since forever, even on our old Quest 2s.  Please fix.  Thanks.

Hey Brian_Clementoni! Thank you so much for taking the time to leave your review! Its awesome to hear that ForeVR Pool has become a prominent fixture in your day to day. We will share your feedback on the 9 ball break noise with the rest of the team. 
Game on! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: Jhbgyuhh
Plays nothing like pool. No youtube tv. Would like a refund. Have tried all the other ForeVr apps. And they are really fun. But the pool mechanics are wonky. Really really ruins pool for me.

Hello LKiNG7, We would love to know more about your experience and what you found lacking. We want all of our players to have the best time playing and your feedback would be very insightful! Please let our support team know your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: This could be such a good game, but the handling of the cue is unnatural and playing of the shot is odd. I tried another pool game and holding and using the cue was the same as real life, unfortunately this game isn't ☹️

developer_response.body:
Hello SilentPukeko, We are bummed to hear that you are not fully enjoying the way the cue is held. We will share your experience with the rest of the team. If there is anything else we can do to improve your experience please let our support team know here. 
(link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)

reviews.review_description: This game is fun, and I really want to play more of it but when I lock on with the triggers it always moves me to another position. My hands in real life are not lined up with my hands in the game. It's frustrating and being someone who often plays pool in real life, it feels unnatural. I have video to better explain what I mean if there is somewhere I can post/send it for you to see.

developer_response.body:
Hello TrollSlayer325! Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We would love to see the video you have, please send it to our support team here! 
(link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
Thank you, Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
I think - especially in Mixed Reality - it's really weird (annoying) that as soon as I lock my front  hand by pulling the trigger, the whole table zooms in... 🙄

Developers, can you please add a feature where I can lock my front hand in place, and then I can freely move the cue stick with my other hand, just like in real life? And hit the ball without locking my other hand... 🤔 I don't want to see any predicted ball paths at all.

In Mixed Reality I'd like to get a real life experience if possible. 😎 If you'll do this, I'll give 5 stars for sure!! 😊👌

developer_response.body:
Hey benkesmith! When you are setting up the game rules within the hall you can turn off aim lanes and then you will not see predicted ball paths! You might enjoy one handed mode as an alternative to locking in both hands. 
For the MR mode did you try turning off table rotation before going into the mode? 
We would love to hear more about your experience and how we could improve it. Please reach out to our support team here. 
(link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
Thanks! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: It forces a different view when shooting
reviews.review_description: Warning to anyone thinking of getting this game. You cannot simply hit balls naturally from any angle. Once you're ready to shoot and hold the trigger it suddenly jumps to a different view. And you're holding the stick by the tip instead of the back. You cannot change this in settings. It's like the devs never even played pool before lol It's a shame because the game looks great. If they just got rid of the trigger holding aspect and let you freely hit balls from any natural position then this would be so much better. You can walk around the table and see the perfect shot, line up your angle, but then you MUST press the trigger to get into "shooting mode" and this is exactly where the game miserably fails and takes you out of the immersion.

developer_response.body:
Hello UltraVeloce, Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We will be discussing your feedback with the rest of the team.
We recommending adjusting your height as that should help keep a similar view when you go into shot mode. We strive to make the best virtual pool experience and we hope you can give it another shot! 
If there is anything else we can do to turn your experience around please let us know here! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) 
-Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
This could be so much better. Firstly the annoying AI characters. Doing a stupid celebration after every single shot is not needed. Even worse is when they are still doing it on your shot and hanging over the edge of the table in your line of sight. The table height needs to be more adjustable to make it feel better. The way your character holds the cue isn't realistic and doesn't make for a realistic cueing action. The actual game physics are good. It has a lot of potential but needs more work.

Update in response to the developers response: in regards to your comment about the cueing action and hand position on the cue then I don't agree. If you check out black hole pool they do it perfectly well with no issues.

Update: the annoying character celebrations have gone. The table height is more adjustable. The cueing is better however still doesn't feel natural with your cueing hand too close to your bridging hand. Feels more like your poking at the ball.

developer_response.body: Hey kevbez1969, Thank you for reading our response! We will discuss your feedback with the rest of the team! If you have any other feedback you'd like to share please let us know here (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) Thanks! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: cue is not held in hand corectly
reviews.review_description: my first experience with the game sucks,  lining up the cue to the ball and then pressing the front hand trigger and the cue goes way back behind my front hand.  i had to bring my back hand almost all the way to my front hand to see the tip of the cue.  and to hit the ball my back hand bumped into my front hand it was so close.  Also when the AI's make a shot they are so smug not like playing in a real game at all.  no one in a pool hall would be so smug when sinking a ball.  RUDE!!

developer_response.body:
Hello moose4098, thank you for sharing your feedback on hand placement on the cue with us. We have made strides with the virtual hands on the cue feeling more realistic while still accommodating the headsets front sensors. We will definitely be discussing your experience with the rest of the the team. Also, the bots all have different personalities so they should react differently, regardless we will share your thoughts with the team! 
Thanks again for your feedback! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
I own both of these Quest games (the latter is only available via the AppLab) and have completed the single level campaigns and enjoyed both games. Although, now that I have completed the campaigns, Black Hole Pool is the only pool game for me, and it’s the game I would recommend to others. ForeverPool’s advantage is the five environments and the different bot avatars sitting around within them. Initially it made the environments more inviting. But after a while when you get used to them, and your focus is mostly on the pool table while using the app, then the differences become more apparent / important. Black Hole Pool has almost photo realistic graphics and ball phsyics. ForeverPool is a cartoony and gets worse when you move about the pool room. The ball physics just aren't right and cue control is not as good as Black Hole Pool. 
The ForeverPool movement controls are quite annoying too, how it deliberately snaps you to the pool table when you’re close to it. Black Hole Pool controls and movement are flawless. 
Finally, Black Hole Pool has a giant video screen next to the table and an intuitive interface. Playing my fav music videos, and seeing them on a big screen in between turns, makes playing the game even better.

developer_response.body:
Hello Sydney_Robster! Thank you for sharing your experience with us! While it is a bummer to hear that you prefer a different game we do want to thank you for giving our game your time! As an early player of our game you have already seen a couple of updates be released and we will continue to update ForeVR Pool and expand the experience. A big facet of upcoming and future updates will be addressing player feedback. We have near plans for addressing movement around the table too! 
Our current ball physics allows players to do trick shots and add a variety of spin to their shots, and we would love to know more about how we can your experience. Please send us a note to our support team so we can chat further (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) Thank you! 
We're not sure if you have noticed but in each hall and lobby there is a jukebox where you can play your own music too! 
We strive to make the best virtual experience possible and we want to sincerely thank you for your feedback! We hope you have an awesome new year! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: Did you guys play any pool?
reviews.review_description: So physics for the balls are good and even the English you can add is pretty good even if a bit heavy. The environments seem cool. The wacky cues are pretty cool too. Setting stakes is awesome. The social factor is on point. The problems are coming up to the table and taking position. I got used to it after a bit. The issue that I find glaring and difficult to deal with is the way the game forces you to hold your cue. Nobody in any pool hall I've been in (I've frequented a few daily for a few years) ever chokes up on the back end of the cue.... ypur hands are almost never supposed to be a half a foot apart. I would gladly give this game 5 stars if not for this. You should be able to change your hands position on the back of the cue to be farther back. I'm going to leave my review at 2 stars as this issue makes the game feel unnatural which is not the goal of any physics based VR game. I'll change if the devs fix this issue in an update. Just hope it happens quick before people begin refunding as this issue might be enough for some to do so.

developer_response.body:
Hello OleKingCrow, thank you for sharing your feedback. We totally played a lot of pool! We will be utilizing player feedback in future updates! We did have to move the hands slightly closer than you might do in real life to ensure optimal tracking with the headset, and we actually made a article that went into some of the challenges of adapting real life pool into a virtual experience. We'd love for you to check it out! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool)  
We will be discussing your experience with the rest of the team. If you have any other feedback you would like to share please let us know! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
-Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
so much about this game is brilliant, but the geometry of how your vr arms hold the cue is soooo bad it sort of ruins the whole thing.
if you look at how the AI opponents take a shot their elbow is above their head like they are horribly deformed, or have just had their whole are dislocated. it's ridiculous. and that's how your avatar has to hit the ball too.

bizarre.

please fix this, cause the rest of the game is brilliant.

developer_response.body: Hello there beautron! We do have plans on addressing this in the near future! We understand that sometimes they can look a bit "pretzel" like. Great to hear that there are other aspects of the game you are enjoying though! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
This whole concept is fantastic, the gameplay, the presentation. It is all so let down by the way you are forced to hold the cue. It really detracts from the experience and makes the game much harder to play as it's so alien to the real thing.
This is something that really does need to be addressed to bring this game to where it should be.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback with us DPercy79. We did have to move the hands slightly closer than you might do in real life to ensure optimal tracking with the headset, and we actually made a article that went into some of the challenges of adapting real life pool into a virtual experience. We'd love for you to check it out! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool)
We will be utilizing player feedback, like yours, in future updates! Happy Holidays! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: I really wanted to like this but you may as well just go and play pool at your local bar.  The graphics are cool but the gameplay is let down by completely unrealistic controls and movement.  I get the bowling, its similar movement to the real thing, same with the darts game.  I don't get the point of cornholing so I gave that a miss but as a cue sports fan (especially snooker) I really wanted to like this but the way you hold the cue is ridiculous.  Like how a kid holds it when first trying.  Not for me this one sadly.

developer_response.body: Hello Trooby! Thank you for sharing your feedback and experience with us. We are actively working on ways of addressing movement around the table and the hand position on the cue. If you have any other feedback you'd like to share please reach out to us here (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new). Happy Holidays, Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: Could be a 5 with some control fixes
reviews.review_description:
This game could easily be a 5-star game and I will update my review if it gets the needed tweaks, but I find it currently unplayable as is due to poor controls. 

Firstly, shooting is plain awful.  You have to make shots essentially in boxing stance with both hands in front like someone who has never even played pool before.  I suggest the devs copy some of their competitors.  While they require hands to be in front when lining up a shot (for ir cams), this changes once the position is locked in.  As the user moves their back hand away from the table, at some point (presumably detected with accelerometers), the cue stick stops moving back (or moves back only a little) and the motion repositions the back hand instead.  This allows the user to contact the cue ball much earlier in their stroke while their hand is still beside their body once they swing forward again. 

The second issue is movement around the table.  No matter how carefully I try to move the sticks only-sideways, on one controller I cannot do so without accidentally popping backwards.  The other is slightly better but still way too sensitive.  Even still, my view jumps wildly from side to side when rotating when I would expect the center of the table to stay in one place in the view.  Finally, moving in jumps at all is not great, as one needs finer control to visually line up a shot.  I suggest doing something like one competitor by allowing one to "grab" the table with the grab buttons and move the controllers sideways to smoothly rotate around to an arbitrary angle. 

Lastly, downward movement to the table could use some help.  I suggest animating the transition to make it less disorienting.  More importantly, please add options to set the "low" height or disable lowering entirely so a user can use their own body movement to shoot from a natural position if they so choose.  Thanks, and hope to see updates soon.

developer_response.body: Hello TealVince, thank you for sharing your experience and leaving a very detailed review! We will be discussing your experience with the rest of the team. We will be utilizing player feedback in future updates and we sincerely hope you stick with us! Some tips on movement in the meantime, please note that you will move away from the table if you press the thumbstick backwards. We also encourage you try using smooth locomotion while around the table. This is just the beginning for ForeVR Pool and we thank you again for your feedback! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: A great start but not there yet.
reviews.review_description:
A lot of it is great. I'll go right to what really needs improved:

(1) Others have mentioned this already: the placement of your hands for the cue stick is unnatural and feels nothing like real-life pool. I do understand the possibility of tracking problems if the draw hand goes too far away, but other pool apps on Quest (Black Hole) have managed to do it without any issues. The way this has been implemented feels like you're shooting pool on a toy table with a 12-inch ruler for a cue stick.

(2) I dislike how you are forced into the table-level view with each shot. I also really dislike how you often get embedded into the middle of the table due to this mechanic.

I know it's challenging to bring real-life things into VR, but pool was already solved BRILLIANTLY by SportsBar VR on Rift. It's clear this app was studied in making ForeVR, because the movement ring around the table is just like SportsBar's, and the aiming mechanics are very similar. So I say: don't stop there. SportsBar nailed the entire thing, from movement mechanics to aiming mechanics to table positioning and navigation. Please just copy their implementations whole-hog and shamelessly. I'm not joking. Their hulking movement mechanic was *perfect* for an app like this; aiming was great, precise and natural; cue draw was realistic; you didn't need to stand inside the table if you didn't want to. Just do what they did. Buy the rights to their app if necessary. Please. :)

developer_response.body: Hello SwirlyMaple, thank you so much for sharing your honest and detailed feedback with us. We will be discussing your feedback and experience with the rest of the team. We plan utilizing player feedback, like yours, in all of our updates! If you have any other feedback you'd like to share please send it to us at (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: This game force you to hold the cue in a very strange way. You have to hold the cue in fixed position and it makes the cue like 30cm long. Can’t play with this condition. Also there is no option for adjust table height. Automated height control also restrict freedom of approach. The physics is exaggerated and is too casual. I saw this situation from early stage of Forevr Bowling. But the bowling is better game now so I expect Pool to be improved as well. I am refunding and buy again when the time is come.

developer_response.body: We're bummed to hear that you had a bit of a rocky start vanz.kim. There were certainly some challenges we had to overcome when adapting pool to a fun and realistic virtual pool experience. We did write an article that goes into this topic a little deeper that we would love for you to check out! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool) We will be discussing your feedback and experience with the rest of the team. We sincerely hope you give the game another chance! - Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: I very much wanted to like this game, but it doesn't feel right.  Moving into position to take a shot is cumbersome using controllers. When taking a shot, it feels like I'm laying on top of the table and drawing the pool cue towards my chest instead of alongside my hip as I do in RL. There's no way that i know of to  move your dominant hand along the stick for a more comfortable grip. Graphics are good, like all their games, but this is far from a RW experience, IMO.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your honest feedback RetroRocker, there were certainly some challenges we had to overcome when adapting pool to a fun and realistic virtual pool experience. We did write an article that goes into this topic a little deeper that we would love for you to check out! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool) We will be discussing your feedback and experience with the rest of the team. - Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: Game seems ok but my back hand is too far forward so is very awkward to play. Unless you can change your hand position on the cue then to me it’s flawed. Was hoping for so much better

developer_response.body:
Hello Rotorgoat, thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We plan on addressing player concerns in future updates. In the meantime we would love for you to check out this article we wrote concerning hand position on the cue and some of the challenges of adapting real life pool into a virtual experience. (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool) 
Thank you again for sharing your feedback with us! Happy Holidays! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
I was really looking forward to this game as I love to play pool in RL.  I really have to appreciate that they have done a great job with the physics of ball movement.  Unfortunately,  I really hate the way they have handled player placement  and stick movement.   They place you in the middle of the table almost eye level with the ball and you are holding the stick six inches apart.  They place you so low that your hands have to be at eye level to make your shot, otherwise your stick is obstructed by the table. In comparison to RL pool, this is very uncomfortable.   Again, the ball physics are wonderful.  I just wish the player placement was a little more natural.  

I would also like a solo practice option.  Many times I like to just shoot to practice shots.  I don't like being forced into a game against an opponent.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your honest feedback Tmeskell, it's awesome to hear that you play pool in real life! There were certainly some challenges we had to overcome when adapting pool to a fun and realistic virtual pool experience. We did write an article that goes into this topic a little deeper that we would love for you to check out! (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10661141213197-Adapting-Real-Life-Pool-To-Virtual-Pool) We will be discussing your feedback and desire to have a practice mode with the rest of the team. - Team ForeVR
